title: What collection to get
wikitext:
[]: What collection to get?             Quote Post by alexis8 » Fri Mar 06, 2015 6:58 pm So not that long ago something made me think about NGE again and it really made me want to watch the series again. Once upon a time, I had a complete collection of all the original release DVDs, however, almost 15 years have passed since then and numerous moves and transitions have sadly made that collection not so complete. I want to watch the entire series again and I'm wondering what collection I should get, and from where. I know the Blu-ray is slated to be released in Japan in August but with no news on a US release I'm not sure I want to wait to get that (but I'm sure I will be getting it eventually). I know there are a couple of different collections, but all of them seem pretty hard to find so that also factors into the decision. So, living in the US, what collection should I get and where should I get it from? Thanks everyone!

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Mar 06, 2015 7:24 pm The so-called "Perfect Collection" isn't - it's the DVD equivalent of the original VHS videos. The "Platinum Collection" is the remastered version of the series; any of its packagings is fine (I think the thin-pack is a slightly higher video bit rate than the others, but frankly it's no big deal). The English version of the films was licensed by a different company, so has to be got separately; it also has appallingly bad video quality - tough, that's how it is. They've all been out of print for ages. Just go on Amazon and eBay to look for secondhand sets you can justify the price of. But beware of boolegs. There's a recent thread that discusses how to identify them. Here's another thread about what to buy: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/17214/Help-Purchasing-Neon-Genesis-Evangelion/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sat Mar 07, 2015 5:06 pm +1 on the rec for the Platinum Collection. I wouldn't hold my breath waiting for the Blu-ray release, especially if you're looking for the dub. These titles navigate their way through a tangled thicket of rights acquisition, distribution deals, script translation, voice casting, post production, and so forth that can take years and years to complete. Just look at the timing on the dubbed Rebuild films, for example. Still no sign of the dubbed Q, despite the fact that the Japanese version came out in 2013. And there's a much clearer business case for the Rebuild films on home video here than for yet another version of the original series, which is almost 20 years old at this point. So if Rebuild takes eons, Eva Classic will take eons longer. Or at least I'd guess.

[]:             Quote Post by alexis8 » Sat Mar 07, 2015 5:46 pm Yea I agree on not waiting for the Blu-rays. I dont care about dubbing, I prefer subs but still, just getting that is no small feat. I would say a year or so at absolute best, and many years at worst. The platinum collection does include death and rebirth along with end of eva? I hate going back to DVDs, Ive moved my entire movie collection to BRD but I dont have much choice on this one *sigh*. Thanks for the help everyone! Now if I can just find a copy, in good condition, for less than the cost of selling my kidneys and spleen on the black market...

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sat Mar 07, 2015 6:23 pm If memory serves (it's been awhile), thin-box Platinum does not contain Death & Rebirth, EoE, etc. It's just the original run of the series and four "director's cut" episodes. I might be mistaken about that. I'll have to check my box set when I get home from the trip I'm on. Or someone with the set ready at hand can chime in sooner! :) Perfect is a more comprehensive collection of materials, but the quality is terrible. If you care at all about A/V quality, I'd get Platinum + the movies. Be warned that you're looking at somewhere north of $200 for a used copy on Amazon, or $300+ for a new copy. eBay has cheaper copies available, but many of them are of dubious provenance. (eBay gives pretty much no fucks about bootlegs or counterfeits; caveat emptor.)

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Mar 07, 2015 6:31 pm The series was licensed by ADV and the films by Manga. Being issued by separate companies, they never appear in the same English-language box. Any English-language set with them all in is a bootleg. Perfect is less than Platinum, as it doesn't even have the DC episodes, though those appear on the Genesis DVDs intended to replace the last two of the Perfect ones. It will be interesting to see whether the new remastering will finally get licensed for English release as a complete set for the first time; but whoever does that then has the business of either getting the rights to the existing dubs from two different companies, or of redubbing the lot...

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sat Mar 07, 2015 11:33 pm pwhodges wrote: It will be interesting to see whether the new remastering will finally get licensed for English release as a complete set for the first time; but whoever does that then has the business of either getting the rights to the existing dubs from two different companies, or of redubbing the lot...

[]:             Quote Post by alexis8 » Tue Mar 24, 2015 2:11 pm so, with platinum perfect settled on...where should I get it? I know there are a decent few options, and none of them are perfect, but what, in your experience, has usually turned out the best?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Mar 24, 2015 2:49 pm alexis8 wrote: so, with platinum perfect settled on...
